Before the revelation about the n word, Furhman did not plead the fifth regarding questions about planting evidence. reply_content: @ronm9357there was never any evidence of Fuhrman planting any evidence anywhere. The defense never brought forth any evidence of that and after LAPD investigated those tapes, they found Fuhrman had widely exaggerated his stories for the screenwriter, who hired him as a consultant for her FICTIONAL book and recorded their conversations. Mark Fuhrman arrived at the crime scene after the discovery of the bodies. Fourteen cops were already at the scene. He then went with 3 other detectives to alert OJ Simpson and that’s when he discovered the blood outside AND inside the Bronco. He then did a perimeter check after Kato informed him of the thumping noise and found the other glove. He checked the house and was dismissed from the case that morning. That was the extent of his involvement with this case. When and how would he have planted any evidence? Not saying LAPD didn’t, they certainly did a sloppy job with the investigation and there were some shenanigans but Mark Fuhrman simply had no chance to do it. With his racist past, he was just an easy scapegoat for the defense who relied on the race card to appeal to the majority Black jury.
